capabilities: Utilize property-based testing frameworks to generate random inputs for software testing, ensuring comprehensive coverage and validation of expected properties.

procedure.action: Initialize property-based testing by configuring a framework like QuickCheck or Hypothesis to generate random inputs based on defined properties; ensure inputs cover edge cases and typical usage scenarios using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Execute tests by running the property-based testing framework, capturing all generated inputs and outputs; ensure each test run logs input-output pairs for auditability using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Assert test results by asserting that all properties hold true across all generated inputs; use assertions to check invariants and expected behaviors, logging any violations by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Log test execution metrics including operation_duration_ms and result_code for each test case; ensure logs are stored in a centralized audit trail for future analysis using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Analyze test coverage by reviewing generated input diversity and property validation results; ensure coverage metrics meet predefined thresholds for input variety and property adherence using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Implement automated property-based testing using random input generation to validate software behavior, ensuring robustness and correctness by defining success criteria and metrics.
title: Automated Property-Based Testing

failureConditions: Test coverage is insufficient when random input generation lacks diversity, causing untested edge cases to remain undetected.
Property violations go unnoticed when assertions are improperly configured, causing incorrect software behavior to persist.
Audit trail is incomplete when logging is inconsistent, causing difficulty in tracing test execution and results.
metrics: test results by asserting that all properties hold true across all generated inputs
